trajectory_summary: The call was effectively a qualification dead-end from the moment Carol disclosed existing solar and a scam-framing of the lead source, but there was a genuine window at 01:28 when Carol revealed she wanted to expand her system if costs were covered — a real motivation Holland did not convert. Holland's reactive, factual-correction approach closed that window rather than opening it. The call ended with a polite mutual close, no appointment, and no cross-sell routing despite a clear add-panels signal.
outcome_dispute: v1 outcome tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the correct tag is no_appointment / disqualified_existing_system. Carol has existing solar (Solar Energy World), expressed no interest in a proposal, and ended the call. There is no booking, no tentative appointment, and no callback scheduled.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Yes. But I wanted it to be covered by the state.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Carol handed Holland the call's only real opening — a disclosed motivation with a single stated barrier — and Holland answered it with a factual explanation that closed the window. The objection_inversion move (reframe the cost Carol is already paying vs. the incremental panel cost) was directly available and unused. This is the codex's #1 daily coaching contribution with zero observed instances across the corpus. Holland needs to see the specific move, not a general principle: 'What you can't afford is the bill your current system isn't covering' — then pause and let Carol respond.

key_moments.quote: Yes. All I wondered is if if, our solar would be covered. That's all I was requesting and not a phone call to install some.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carol is signaling a consent-expectation gap before the free-solar framing even lands: she submitted a ZIP-code eligibility check, not a request for a sales call. This is a scam-anxiety signal upstream of the 'free solar' objection. Holland did not acknowledge the gap — no 'I hear you, you were just checking eligibility' bridge before moving into the pitch. The closest codex protocol is how_did_you_get_my_number: specificity reduces suspicion, vagueness increases it. Holland's silence on Carol's consent framing left the suspicion unaddressed.

key_moments.quote: Solar is just a scam, but I appreciate your call.
key_moments.stewart_read: Carol's scam label is a soft close-out, not a hard hang-up — 'I appreciate your call' signals she is still being polite and is not hostile. The rebuttals.changed_mind strategy (curiosity creation, not argument) was available here: 'I totally get that — and honestly, the way that site was worded, I'd think the same thing. Can I ask you one quick question before I let you go?' Holland instead moved directly to the orphaned-flow question at 01:17, which was the right instinct but skipped the rapport repair that would have made Carol more receptive. The codex's hello_objection protocol does not apply here — its timing_constraint is first_30_seconds_only, and this is at 01:13 after substantive exchange.

key_moments.quote: Well, do you already have solar, Carol?
key_moments.stewart_read: Holland's orphaned-flow pivot here is schema-compliant and the best move in the call. Rather than defending Ion against the scam label, Holland asked a qualifying question that surfaced Carol's existing system — which then opened the add-panels thread at 01:28. This is the call's one moment of strategic intent: Holland recognized that Carol's engagement with solar (even if frustrated) warranted a qualification check rather than an immediate close-out.

key_moments.quote: Yes. But I wanted it to be covered by the state.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's highest-value missed moment. Carol has disclosed a real motivation — she wants to expand her existing system, and cost coverage is the only barrier. The objection_inversion move was directly available: the cost Carol is already paying on her current bill (or the gap her existing system doesn't cover) is the actual expense, not the incremental panel cost. Holland instead explained partial state incentives on Carol's terms, which confirmed the barrier rather than reframing it. The codex flags objection_inversion as the #1 daily coaching contribution with zero observed instances across the corpus — this call is another data point in that pattern.
